formations_blocks: Australia with an extra man in midfield
adjustments: Paraguay shifted from a back four to a back five
Mauricio replaced Maidana at the break
Mauricio introduced to help Paraguay circulate the ball in midfield and act as a metronome
Australia instructed to continue with the same approach ('more of the same')
key_matchups: Volpato finding joy on the right against Paraguay's reshaped midfield
Irankunda vs Paraguay defenders in the box
phases.summary: The half-time picture was shaped by Paraguay’s switch from a back four to a back five, which gave Australia an extra midfield man and helped Volpato find space on the right. After the break Paraguay responded with a more assertive approach, introducing Mauricio to improve midfield circulation and create more transition threat, while Australia were warned to avoid being caught out in a game increasingly decided around halfway.

adjustments: Australia made six changes from the previous match and all of them delivered
Volpato started on the right flank and linked with Bos behind him
Australia’s usual left wingback swapped sides and became their most dangerous player in the unfamiliar position
key_matchups: Australia’s back three vs Paraguay’s attack
Bos supporting Volpato on the right flank
phases.summary: The closing phase was shaped by Australia’s organised back-three structure and a disciplined defensive effort, with Circati especially effective in shutting down Enciso whenever he escaped pressure. Tony Popovic’s reshuffled side was tactically flexible, with Volpato thriving on the right and the usual left wingback switching sides to become Australia’s main attacking threat, while Paraguay’s approach centred heavily on isolating Enciso without finding a breakthrough.
